verdict.answer_first: B leads with the command in the first span: “`lsof -i :3000`”; A first says “On macOS:” then code, still direct but slightly slower.
verdict.decisiveness: B keeps one main recommendation, then labels the rest “close second”; A adds “A couple of notes” and an “Alternative,” which feels more like a mini-survey.
verdict.actionability: B is easier to act on because it gives the exact command and kill path in 2 compact blocks; A is useful but adds edge-case notes before they are needed.
